title: A century of coherent states
abstract: During the century of existence of the notion of coherent states, either linear or nonlinear, several schemes for their construction, theoretical or experimental, have been developed. Generally, the mathematical structure of coherent states depends on the choice of ladder operators, and consequently on the structure constants. In this paper, we propose a way to construct generalized coherent states for anharmonic oscillators that is based on a diagonal operator ordering technique (DOOT) applied to generalized hypergeometric functions, that is, on some of the most general special functions. These states are generated by the action of a pair of ladder operators, the creation and the annihilation, whose ordered normal product is equal to the dimensionless Hamiltonian of the quantum system. In addition, the action of these operators is easy to find if the expression for the dimensionless energy eigenvalues is known.
summary: This paper develops an advanced mathematical theory to construct generalized coherent states for anharmonic quantum oscillators. It introduces the Diagonal Operator Ordering Technique (DOOT) to systematically handle nonlinear Hamiltonians using special functions like hypergeometric and Fox-Wright functions. This framework allows physicists to analyze systems beyond simple harmonic motion, providing tools to characterize their quantum state properties.
main_problem: The primary goal is to develop a systematic, generalized mathematical framework for constructing coherent states applicable to anharmonic quantum oscillators.
main_result: Generalized coherent states can be constructed using the Diagonal Operator Ordering Technique (DOOT) applied to hypergeometric functions, providing an extension beyond standard harmonic oscillator definitions.
method: The paper employs advanced mathematical techniques including DOOT, analysis of energy eigenvalue spectra ($	ilde{e}_n$), and utilizing generalized special functions like the Fox-Wright function for normalization.
model_or_system: The focus is on quantum oscillators ranging from the simple harmonic oscillator (linear) to anharmonic systems like Morse or Pöschl-Teller potentials (nonlinear). The formalism extends to continuous spectra and thermal environments.
key_observables: Mandel parameter ($\mathcal{M}$), expectation values of operator products, and generalized structure constants ($ho_{np}^q$).
important_parameters: The functional form of the dimensionless energy eigenvalues ($	ilde{e}_n$) dictates the specific mathematical structure of the resulting coherent state.
main_assumptions: It assumes that rules analogous to normal ordering (IWOP) can be systematically extended via DOOT to handle nonlinear Hamiltonians and operators.
figures_summary: The notes detail numerous equations representing generalized hypergeometric functions, Meijer G-functions, and specific formulas for the Mandel parameter in various spectral regimes.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing the necessity of generalization (comparing linear/nonlinear systems), then detailing the DOOT technique, deriving the general mathematical form using special functions (Fox-Wright/hypergeometric), and finally applying this framework to analyze moments and physical observables like photon statistics.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant for theoretical physicists working on open quantum systems or quantum optics because it provides a rigorous mathematical tool (DOOT) to define generalized coherent states for complex, anharmonic potentials where standard methods fail.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
